name: REO Speedwagon
description:
'''REO Speedwagon''' (originally stylized as '''R.E.O. Speedwagon'''), or simply '''REO''', was an American [[Rock music|rock]] band formed in [[Champaign, Illinois|Champaign, Illinois]], in 1967. The band cultivated a following during the 1970s and achieved significant commercial success throughout the 1980s. Their best-selling album, ''[[Hi Infidelity]]'' (1980), contained four US Top 40 hits and sold more than 10 million copies.

REO Speedwagon sold more than 40 million records and charted 13 Top 40 hits, including the number ones "[[Keep On Loving You (song)|Keep On Loving You]]" and "[[Can't Fight This Feeling]]". After 57 years of activity, including sixteen studio albums and numerous lineup changes (with keyboardist Neal Doughty as the only constant member), REO Speedwagon retired as a full-time band in December 2024. The band has since made occasional one-off live appearances.
